title: Verify current Meshtastic DeviceMetadata schema fields against Zephyr codec
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-t2hn.15 review found the active Zephyr C DeviceMetadata encoder is hand-coded against the pinned schema and lacks some fields present in the Rust prototype, such as platform_type. What needs to be done: compare current upstream and pinned Meshtastic protobuf DeviceMetadata fields with Zephyr codec.c, decide whether to stay pinned or add fields, and add tests for required app-facing fields. Acceptance criteria: schema comparison is recorded with commit SHAs; Zephyr codec either encodes every required field or documents safe omission; native_sim tests validate required metadata fields.
notes:
Reviews: first pass Beauvoir and Rawls clean except Beauvoir residual no-extra-fields risk; fixed by adding schema-range guard. Second/third passes found duplicate-field and partial Bluetooth-false branch coverage gaps; fixed by duplicate rejection and shared full assertion helper. Final independent reviews Sartre and Pasteur clean: no remaining findings; both confirmed no-extra/no-duplicate scan, all 12 fields checked for both Bluetooth states, and schema/doc accuracy.
Implemented schema verification evidence and test coverage for DeviceMetadata. Schema comparison: checked-in LICHEN revision 908b6d0f87aae73a248a30d0bb49e01c6f998255; pinned meshtastic/protobufs 032b7dfd68e875c4323e6ac67590c6fc616b1714; current meshtastic/protobufs master 9cb134be322dd7122e80d49b17dad9a213ff752e. Pinned and current upstream DeviceMetadata are identical: fields 1 firmware_version, 2 device_state_version, 3 canShutdown, 4 hasWifi, 5 hasBluetooth, 6 hasEthernet, 7 role, 8 position_flags, 9 hw_model, 10 hasRemoteHardware, 11 hasPKC, 12 excluded_modules; no platform_type field exists. Zephyr already emits all 12 fields; added native_sim ztest coverage requiring only fields 1-12, rejecting duplicate fields, and validating all 12 values for Bluetooth true and false.
Validation on EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 in clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn24-validate, EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr, Zephyr v3.7.0 SDK 0.16.8. Direct command: west build -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -d build/meshtastic_codec-t2hn24-final --pristine -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn24-validate/lichen && west build -d build/meshtastic_codec-t2hn24-final -t run. Result: PASS, meshtastic_codec 22/22 tests, including test_metadata_payload_matches_current_schema. Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_ble -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -T lichen/tests/app_interface -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-t2hn24-final --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn24-validate/lichen. Result: PASS 5/5 configurations, 112/112 test cases, 0 skipped, 0 failures/errors, 0 warnings in 103.06s. Superseded earlier Twister run also passed but final evidence is the t2hn24-final run.
close_reason: Completed: compared pinned/current Meshtastic DeviceMetadata schemas, documented SHAs and 12-field contract, verified Zephyr emits all required fields, added native_sim coverage for all fields/no extras/no duplicates across Bluetooth states, passed reviews and EC2 native_sim/Twister.
